decision_target: Decide whether a fixed controller-flow loop, per-exec tags, awaited promises, a current runtime resource, and explicit ports cover bounded issue queue work without start, spawn, WorkerRegistry, task, session, or a public pool.
source_of_truth: The v2 eight-case behavior probe accepted as intact by validator-dkr-4-v2, the v3 modeled graph, direct root replay, corrected DKR-2 acceptance artifact, frame v2 ten-minute freshness contract, and absolute public checkpoint checker.
read_method: Run bash .okra/runs/issue-triage-session-20260715/workers/dkr-4-v3/replay.sh from the feature worktree root and require eight queue cases, five ports, three controller edges, concurrency two, one activation per lease, zero crossed session values, zero forbidden surfaces, zero hidden effects, nine hashes, corrected DKR-2 state, ten-minute wall reads, and a blocked gate pending independent v3 validation.

confidence_probability_update.reason: V3 preserves the accepted bounded behavior and repairs the four explicit v2 validator rejections without changing the composition contract.

questions_answered: The root replay invokes Node directly from the shell and uses no nested child Node process.
The accepted v2 behavior remains eight of eight cases, five ports, three controller edges, concurrency two, one activation per lease, zero crossed session values, and graceful join in every case.
The modeled surface adds no worker, WorkerRegistry, pool, start, spawn, task, or session Lite export.
The surface checker scans modeled-surface.json rather than its own fixture strings.
DKR-2 is accepted_as_reducing_discovery, while implementation remains unauthorized.
Forced close depends on later Lite implementation of DKR-2 and remains outside ordinary queue behavior.
The checkpoint and all eight wall reads use the frame ten-minute maximum age.
The absolute public contract checker is part of the root replay.
questions_unanswered: A separate validator has not replayed the v3 candidate.
DKR-2 has no Lite implementation and no queue implementation may depend on forced close today.
Provider-specific acknowledgement idempotency, lease fencing, receive shutdown, and distributed fairness remain adapter concerns.
decision: candidate only; bounded controller behavior remains intact and all v2 rejection causes are repaired, but acceptance remains pending independent v3 validation
flag_if_missing_or_stale: cannot: fail closed when any behavior case, graph edge, evidence hash, DKR-2 state, ten-minute freshness read, public contract check, or independent v3 review is missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted

repairs_prior_rejections.repair: The v3 root replay invokes each Node checker directly from the shell and contains no nested child process.
The contract reads accepted_as_reducing_discovery and separately records implementation_authorized false.
The checkpoint and every wall use max_age 10m from frame v2.
The replay records and invokes the absolute installed checker and contract paths.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Five required ports, three controller edges, and the v3 modeled surface
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Run the v3 replay and count effects or child calls outside required ports and controller edges.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Five modeled effect edges and the v3 source audit
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Require every modeled effect edge to carry via_required_port true and every child flow edge to be declared.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: The accepted two-session one-Scope behavior trace
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Compare session, issue, lease, observation, and result ownership values across the two activation executions.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: In-memory queue and timer port adapters inside the behavior probe
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Count real GitHub, queue, process, network, or database writes during replay.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: Eight queue behaviors, fixed loop, modeled public surface, and accepted DKR-2 boundary
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Replay bounded admission, backpressure, join, terminal ownership, and shutdown without a public lifecycle or pool surface.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: V3 writer checkpoint without a v3 validator record
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Require a separate validator to replay the v3 candidate before acceptance.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: V3 packet authority, nine evidence hashes, governed worker report, and store verification
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Compare writes with workers/dkr-4-v3, resolve every hash, append through the absolute helper, and verify the run store.

active_anti_goal_verification.source_of_truth: The four v2 rejections, their v3 repairs, pending independent review, and blocked downstream gate
active_anti_goal_verification.read_method: Confirm every prior rejection is cited and repaired, no writer acceptance is recorded, and downstream remains blocked.

wall_gate.reason: V3 is writer evidence only. Independent v3 validation is still required, and DKR-2 implementation remains unauthorized.

empty_result_policy: An empty result is a valid non-accepted decision that still carries a replayable probe trace.
checkpoint_rejection_policy: Superficial, tautological, false-positive, fabricated, unsupported, and single-LLM-truth checkpoints are rejected. Missing, stale, wrong-source, non-replayable, or contradicted evidence fails closed and opens a blocking flag.

worked_checkpoint_trace.decision: candidate only pending independent v3 replay

role_grammar: CKRs are measurable contribution context, not worker work.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
